questions.clue: This Hawaiian volcano, one of the most active in the world, has been erupting nearly continuously since 1983.
The 79 AD eruption of this volcano buried Pompeii and Herculaneum under volcanic ash.
This Indonesian volcano's 1883 eruption was heard over 3,000 miles away and caused global temperature drops.
The eruption of this Icelandic volcano in 2010 disrupted European air travel for weeks due to its massive ash cloud.
The 1815 eruption of this Indonesian volcano was the largest in recorded history, causing the Year Without a Summer in 1816.
This British band released the iconic albums Abbey Road and Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band.
Freddie Mercury was the legendary frontman of this band known for Bohemian Rhapsody.
This guitarist, known for playing left-handed on a right-handed Stratocaster, headlined Woodstock in 1969.
Pink Floyd's 1973 album that spent over 900 weeks on the Billboard 200 chart features a prism on its cover.
choices: The Dark Side of the Moon

questions.clue: This drummer, famous for his work with The Who, was one of rock's first to use a double bass drum setup and died in 1978.
This is the official currency of Japan, symbolized by the yen sign.
The pound sterling is the official currency of this island nation, the oldest currency still in use.
This South African currency is named after the Witwatersrand ridge, where most of the country's gold deposits were found.
India's currency, the rupee, features this leader on all its banknotes since 1996.
This tiny European principality uses the Swiss franc as its official currency despite not being part of Switzerland.

dailyDouble.clue: This Australian bird builds the most elaborate structures of any animal, decorating its avenue-shaped bower with colorful objects to attract mates.
